learningObjectives: Recognize when a program is installed

plainDefinitions.installer: A program that sets up another program on your computer.
plainDefinitions.Apps & Features: Windows settings page where you manage installed programs.

examples: Removing a game that slows the computer.
analogies: An installer is like a delivery person setting up furniture in your house.
handsOnTasks: Install a simple program from an installer.
Uninstall an old program from Apps & Features.
breakFixTasks: Fix a failed install by rerunning the installer.
ticketIdeas: Computer slow due to too many apps.
automationOpportunities: Later: silent installs and scripted uninstalls.
reviewQuestions: Where do you go to uninstall programs?
How do you know a program installed correctly?
miniGameIdeas: Spot the safe vs unsafe installer.
Order the steps of installing a program.
